extraction.one_line_summary: VERONA Carlos soloed to first-ever WorldTour win from long breakaway while ROGLIČ Primož attacked with 2km remaining at Vaujany to take yellow, catching Verona with 400m left but finishing 2nd.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo sacrificed ELISSONDE Kenny with mid-climb acceleration to thin breakaway before summit, revealing aggressive WorldTour breakaway disruption tactics.
ROGLIČ Primož delayed final attack until 2km/1.5km mark on Vaujany rather than earlier gradients, demonstrating calculated late-acceleration strategy on summit finishes.
Movistar's VERONA took WorldTour breakthrough at age 29 after career domestique role, showing breakaway specialists often undervalued when liberated from leader support.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: answered knee injury concerns emphatically with furious 2km solo chase, closing 37-second gap to nearly catch Verona at finish
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: finished 3rd in GC group but allowed ROGLIČ's late attack without response, suggesting either tactical acceptance or insufficient form
rider_signals.HAGEN Edvald Boasson: negative: dropped early despite Trek's aggression setting him up for stage win, indicating disappointing climbing form compared to team expectations
rider_signals.ELISSONDE Kenny: positive: launched breakaway-disrupting attack mid-climb for Trek despite appearing dropped, showing tactical sophistication in expendable breakaway sabotage role
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: sprinted hard to follow ROGLIČ's acceleration and finished 4th, demonstrating improved response to explosive GC attacks compared to previous races
rider_signals.HART Tao GEOGHEGAN: negative: lost 48 seconds to winner in GC group, grimacing throughout final climb, signaling concerning form fade on decisive mountain stages
